svg.text: Four Experiment Types — Stage-by-Stage Workflow Comparison
Side-by-Side Progressive Simulation Stages for Drug Synthesis, Analysis, Pharmaceutics, and Pharmacology/Toxicology
Safety: PPE check, fume hood verification
NMR, MS, IR, UV, melting point, elemental analysis
Glassware cleaning, waste segregation, EHS log
USP/ChP method or develop new (ICH Q2)
HPLC, GC, UV-Vis, titration, Karl Fischer
API properties, excipient selection, QbD approach
Route, dose calculation, frequency, vehicle control
⚠ Max tolerated dose / acute toxicity limit
Clinical signs, body weight, behavior, biomarkers
GLP report, IACUC review, AVMA euthanasia
All Experiment Types Converge to Common Output
Experiment Report (IMRaD format) | Score: Std Compliance 40% + Safety 25% + Data Integrity 20% + Efficiency 15% | Capability Radar Chart | Error Log & Improvement Plan
Standards Referenced Across All Four Experiment Types
GMP (2010 Rev.) + 6 Appendices | GLP (2017 Rev.) | NMPA Guidelines | Drug Administration Law
WHO-GMP / cGMP / EU-GMP | OECD-GLP / FDA 21 CFR 58 | ICH Q/S/E Series
GHS Chemical Classification | EHS / OSHA Lab Safety | PPE Requirements | Biosafety Levels BSL-1 to BSL-4 | 3R Principles (Replace, Reduce, Refine) | IACUC Ethics Review | AVMA Euthanasia Guidelines
OpenClaw Skill v1.0 | Four Experiment Type Workflows | MIT License
